key_themes: Record Q1 2026 earnings beat with second-highest quarterly revenues and EPS ever
Strength in equities trading (all-time high) and investment banking/M&A advisory rebound
Solid 19.8% ROE, strong capital returns via buybacks and dividends
Post-earnings stock sell-off despite beats (FICC weakness, profit-taking, broader market)
Constructive 2026 global growth and dealmaking outlook
summary: Goldman Sachs delivered a standout Q1 2026 with $17.23B in net revenues (14% YoY increase, beat estimates), $5.63B net earnings, $17.55 EPS, and 19.8% ROE, driven by record equities trading, higher investment banking fees (especially advisory/M&A), and robust client activity. The firm returned significant capital to shareholders and maintained a strong capital buffer. Despite the blowout results, shares dipped 3–4% post-earnings on mixed FICC performance and high expectations, though longer-term sentiment remains bullish on the diversified franchise and medium-term macro outlook.[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/goldman-sachs-shows-strength-despite-173000181.html)[[4]](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-goldman-sachs-beats-q1-2026-forecasts-stock-dips-93CH-4610771)
sources: 12+ news articles and earnings transcripts (CNBC, Yahoo Finance, GS official release, Motley Fool), 15+ recent X posts (earnings reactions, analyst notes, stock movement discussion), GS 2026 Outlooks and analyst price targets (average ~$900–915, Hold consensus)
